name: Torre de Préjano y Castillo de Herace – circular from Arnedo via the Vía Verde
name_original: Torre de Préjano y Castillo de Herace – circular desde Arnedo por la Vía Verde
region_intro: Touring cycling around Arnedo, located in La Rioja, Spain, features diverse landscapes within the Cidacos Valley. The region is characterized by mountains and unique rock formations of conglomerates and sandstones, creating varied terrain. The Cidacos River carves through the valley, providing picturesque scenery and routes. This area offers extensive trail networks suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Arnedo The most popular touring cycling route is Torre de Préjano y Castillo de Herace – circular from Arnedo via the Vía Verde, an 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route utilizes the Vía Verde, offering views of the surrounding valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo, a moderate 23.9 miles (38.5 km) path. This route follows the Cidacos Greenway, leading towards the thermal baths of Arnedillo. Local touring cyclists also love the Senda de los Cuñados – Ermita de San Marcos loop from Arnedo, a 24.2 miles (39.0 km) trail leading through the mountainous terrain of the Cidacos Valley, often completed in about 3 hours 56 minutes. Touring cycling around Arnedo is defined by the Cidacos Valley, distinctive rock formations, and the Sierra de Arnedo mountains. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy greenways to more challenging mountain paths. The routes in Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Arnedo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain and difficulty levels can I expect on touring cycling routes around Arnedo?
A: Arnedo offers a diverse range of terrain, from the smooth, easy-to-navigate paths of the greenways along the Cidacos River to more challenging mountain trails in the Sierra de Arnedo. You'll encounter varied landscapes featuring unique rock formations of conglomerates and sandstones. While there are a few easy and moderate routes, the majority of touring cycling routes in the area are considered difficult, providing options for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Arnedo?
A: Yes, Arnedo offers some easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners or families. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40006871" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Cliffs of Herce – Cidacos Greenway loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 12.9-mile (20.8 km) trail. The Cidacos Greenway itself provides smooth, pleasant cycling experiences along the river.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I see along the touring cycling routes in Arnedo?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Arnedo offer stunning natural sights. You can explore the picturesque Cidacos Valley, marvel at the distinctive El Picuezo y la Picueza rock formations, or cycle past the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6307839" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Cliffs of Herce&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;thermal baths of Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt; are also a popular destination, combining cycling with relaxation.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible by touring bike near Arnedo?
A: Yes, touring cycling routes can lead you to several historical and cultural sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5336999" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Marcos&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also known for its 'La Rioja Rupestre' aspect, with numerous caves like the Cueva de los Cien Pilares, offering a glimpse into historical cave dwellings.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes available around Arnedo?
A: Yes, Arnedo features several excellent circular touring cycling routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994464384/torre-de-prejano-y-castillo-de-herace-circular-from-arnedo-via-the-via-verde" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Préjano y Castillo de Herace – circular from Arnedo via the Vía Verde&lt;/a&gt;, an 18.2-mile (29.3 km) trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31489798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 23.9-mile (38.5 km) path.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about their experience in Arnedo?
A: The touring cycling routes around Arnedo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the scenic Cidacos Valley, and the variety of routes available, from easy greenways to more challenging mountain paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Arnedo?
A: Arnedo's climate makes it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, with comfortable weather and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be warm, especially in the Cidacos Valley, while winters are milder but can have cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that lead to thermal baths or places for relaxation?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31489798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; is a fantastic route that takes you directly to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;thermal baths of Arnedillo&lt;/a&gt;. This allows you to combine your cycling activity with a relaxing soak in natural thermal springs.

Q: Can I find challenging touring cycling routes with significant elevation gain around Arnedo?
A: Yes, if you're looking for a challenge, Arnedo has several difficult touring cycling routes with substantial elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Turruncún – Castle of Arnedo loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt; covers 31.9 miles (51.4 km) with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering a rewarding experience for advanced cyclists.

Q: Are there options for mountain biking or more technical trails in addition to touring cycling?
A: While this guide focuses on touring cycling, the region around Arnedo is known for its extensive trail networks that cater to various cycling preferences, including mountain biking. The area features a mix of flowy sections, varied slopes, and technical features, making it suitable for both road biking and mountain biking enthusiasts.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available from Arnedo?
A: One of the longer touring cycling routes available from Arnedo is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Turruncún – Castle of Arnedo loop from Arnedo&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 31.9 miles (51.4 km) and is classified as difficult due to its significant elevation changes.
tips: Raised on a small hillock northwest of Préjano, in La Rioja, are the remains of a 15th-century castle. In the current enclosure you can see a small tower 15 meters high.

Castle built around the year 1200 taking advantage of a previous Islamic fortification. Strategically located with a great view of the middle valley of the Cidacos river, the Serbian fortification of surveillance space, as well as being an important point of communication with the other castles and towers in the area.
Near the castle is the Hermitage of El Salvador.

At 525 meters above sea level, located in the middle valley of Cidacos, in the region of La Rioja Baja, is the city of Arnedo.
The fortification from Roman times stands out, which was rebuilt by the Arabs during the occupation in the 9th century. The castle stands on an imposing rocky hill from where it has control of the town and its surroundings.
